assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 79-87 detail each criterion: (1) single license filter PASS, (2) comma-separated filter PASS, (3) invalid license 400 PASS, (4) pagination integration PASS, (5) response shape unchanged PASS. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each mark their respective criterion as 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified), and tests/api/package.rs (created). No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.' Lines 35-40 detail file-by-file scope comparison against task specification.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). No code files were created or modified. The report describes reading and analyzing the diff but contains no evidence of code modification. All outputs are verification/reporting artifacts.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and presents detailed findings. There is no mention of merging, approving, or any automated merge action. The report ends with the note 'This report was generated as part of the verify-pr eval for TC-9101' — it is a read-only verification report presenting findings for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No sensitive patterns detected in added lines. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials found. All additions are application logic and test code.' Lines 58-67 provide detailed scan results across all pattern categories.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file created (tests/api/package.rs) with 4 new test functions; no existing test files modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive.' Lines 113-115 reiterate: 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is a newly created file. It adds 4 new test functions with comprehensive assertions. No existing test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-115 organize detailed analysis under four domain headings: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 33-49, containing Scope Containment PASS, Diff Size PASS, Commit Traceability PASS), 'Security' (lines 57-67, containing Sensitive Pattern Scan PASS), 'Correctness' (lines 69-91, containing CI Status PASS, Acceptance Criteria PASS, Verification Commands N/A), 'Style/Conventions' (lines 93-115, containing Convention Upgrade N/A, Repetitive Test Detection PASS, Test Documentation PASS, Test Change Classification ADDITIVE). All expected verdicts are present under their respective domains.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 35-53): lists each file with its scope classification (Modified vs Created) and expected vs actual file counts. Security (lines 59-67): enumerates each scanned category (passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, .env files, cloud credentials, database credentials). Correctness (lines 77-87): details each of 5 acceptance criteria with specific code references (e.g., 'validate_license_param splits on commas', 'Condition::any() with is_in()', 'Expression::parse(id) from spdx crate') and test names. Style/Conventions (lines 99-115): lists all 4 test functions with their distinct patterns and assesses documentation. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide further per-criterion evidence with code-level detail.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear in the summary table (lines 4-6) before any domain-specific analysis sections (which start at line 29 'Detailed Domain Analysis'), indicating they are determined at the orchestrator level independently from the four domain sub-agents.
